title: Octopus Deploy API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Octopus Deploy API key detected in variable assignment. Octopus Deploy API keys provide full access to deployment automation, release management, project configurations, and infrastructure orchestration. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized users to trigger deployments, access sensitive environment variables, modify deployment processes, and compromise your entire deployment pipeline. Store API keys securely using environment variables or secret management systems.

Octopus Deploy API key detected in X-Octopus-ApiKey header. The X-Octopus-ApiKey header provides authentication to Octopus Deploy servers for API requests. Exposing this header allows unauthorized access to deployments, projects, releases, and sensitive environment configurations. This can lead to unauthorized deployments, data breaches, and infrastructure compromise. Use secure credential storage and avoid hardcoding API headers.

Octopus Deploy CLI command with hardcoded API key detected. The 'octo' command-line tool with --apiKey parameter contains deployment automation credentials. Exposing these commands allows unauthorized deployments, release creation, project modifications, and access to sensitive environment variables. Store API keys in environment variables or secure configuration files.

Octopus Deploy bearer token detected in authorization header. Bearer tokens with API- prefix provide full API access to Octopus Deploy services. Exposing these tokens allows unauthorized deployment triggers, project access, release management, and infrastructure configuration changes. Store tokens securely and rotate immediately if exposed.

Generic Octopus Deploy API key detected in source code. Octopus Deploy API keys starting with 'API-' provide access to deployment automation, release management, and infrastructure orchestration. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized deployments, access to environment variables, project modifications, and potential infrastructure compromise. Use environment variables or secret management systems for credential storage.
